commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens agent runtime tool-schema projection, OpenAI strict-schema diagnostics, effective inventory, doctor warnings, and quarantine logging so unreadable or malformed tool rows are diagnosed without dropping healthy sibling tools.

PR surface: Source +476, Tests +495. Total +971 across 20 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main reads tool rows and descriptor fields directly, while the PR tests model throwing getters/rows that would abort projection before this change. I did not run the failing repro because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human maintainer review remains because the PR is draft/protected and the only residual question is proof sufficiency, not a narrow ClawSweeper repair defect.

target.title: fix(agents): harden runtime tool schema diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary
- Harden runtime tool-schema projection so unreadable or malformed plugin tool rows are quarantined instead of crashing assistant startup.
- Make effective inventory, OpenAI strict-schema diagnostics, active doctor warnings, and quarantine logging tolerate unreadable tool rows/descriptors while preserving healthy sibling tools.
- Scrub the regression fixtures to use synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` names only; this split intentionally excludes the provider-catalog work already covered by https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88351.

Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87141

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: a malformed active plugin tool schema or unreadable tool descriptor can crash runtime/doctor projection before healthy tools are returned.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux runner through the OpenClaw Crabbox wrapper, plus focused local node-wrapper Vitest after the final clean rebase.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts --reporter=verbose`

Evidence after fix: AWS Crabbox `run_24f0252753f1`, lease `cbx_b0bd71cfccae`, exit 0; final head `6c5bafa5e70b18a3c054d84ce3f92648e71592d9` rebased cleanly onto `origin/main` `c94c43d3bb285e28fe922d0a3661f44efa8a7fef` and passed focused post-rebase Vitest, diff check, and scrub.

Observed result after fix: malformed or unreadable `fuzzplugin` rows/descriptors/nested schemas are quarantined or diagnosed, healthy `mockplugin` siblings remain visible, and quarantine logging no longer re-reads unreadable rows.

What was not tested: a live third-party plugin/provider call; Blacksmith Testbox was not used because this shell requires `blacksmith auth login`.

## Verification
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (3 files / 31 tests, post-rebase)
- Earlier focused runtime batch: 5 Vitest shards / 244 tests on this split before the final type-only amend/rebase
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` (`run_24f0252753f1`, `cbx_b0bd71cfccae`, exit 0)
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check` on changed files
- targeted `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on changed files
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- forbidden-name/private-path/conflict-marker scrub over the branch diff and split spec
- autoreview clean after accepted findings were fixed
